description_long: This object consists of two adjacent wooden storage crates, each with a square shape and reinforced edges. The crates are constructed from weathered wooden planks, with visible grain and a slightly mossy or stained lower section, suggesting outdoor or long-term storage use. Both crates feature a prominent circular logo on the front face, reading 'CUNARD WHITE STAR,' and one crate displays an adhesive shipping label on the side. The tops of the crates are flat and appear to be removable or liftable, with faint circular markings indicating possible lid fasteners or access points. The overall construction is robust, suitable for storing or transporting goods, and the crates are large enough to require two hands or mechanical assistance to move. The design and markings imply historical or industrial use, possibly for shipping or warehousing.
description: A pair of weathered wooden storage crates with circular 'CUNARD WHITE STAR' logos and a shipping label, featuring flat removable lids and moss-stained lower edges.
description_view_0: Front view showing both crates side by side, with the 'CUNARD WHITE STAR' logos clearly visible on the front faces.
description_view_1: Rear angled view highlighting the plain wooden backs and the mossy lower sections, with no visible logos or labels.
description_view_2: Front angled view similar to view 0, but with a partial view of the side label on the right crate.
description_view_3: Rear angled view showing the adhesive shipping label on the right crate and the plain wooden backs.
